steps.title: Place the interrupting phrase
steps.content: The phrase "on the other hand" interrupts the clause "Tales in English often begin with 'once upon a time.'" An interrupting transitional phrase inside a clause should be set off with commas.

steps.title: Reject broken punctuation
steps.content: A uses mismatched dash-and-comma punctuation. B creates a fragment after a period. D uses a colon where the sentence needs an interrupting phrase, not an explanation introduced after a complete clause.

steps.title: Set off the transition
steps.content: English, on provides the first comma, completing "English, on the other hand, often begin" with matching commas. The answer is C.
